mapping.a_element: optical lattice hosting the ultracold atoms
mapping.b_element: the discrete lattice of interacting classical degrees of freedom

mapping.a_element: atoms localized at individual lattice sites in the Mott phase
mapping.b_element: spatially localized breather solution on the lattice

mapping.a_element: critical lattice-potential depth (interaction-to-tunneling coupling ratio) that triggers the superfluid-Mott transition
mapping.b_element: critical spatial dimension d_c above which breather energy acquires a positive lower bound

mapping.a_element: excitation gap of the Mott insulator
mapping.b_element: positive lower bound on breather energy

brief_justification: Paper A is a quantum many-body ground-state phase transition governed by the competition between tunneling and on-site repulsion (a Bose-Hubbard coupling ratio driven across a critical value), whereas Paper B is a classical existence/bifurcation result about energy thresholds of nonlinear localized lattice solutions set by the spatial dimension d_c; despite shared vocabulary (lattice, localization, critical value, gap/threshold), the driving machinery differs (interaction-driven quantum phase transition vs. dimension-dependent energy bound on nonlinear breathers), so no coherent role-for-role mapping holds and the listed correspondences are only superficial.
